description.en: A curated list of practical Codex skills for automating workflows across the Codex CLI and API.
description.zh: 实用的 Codex 技能精选列表，用于自动化 Codex CLI 和 API 的工作流。

description.en: TradingAgents: Multi-Agents LLM Financial Trading Framework. Autonomous research, analysis, risk management, and trading roles powered by LLM agents.
description.zh: TradingAgents：多智能体 LLM 金融交易框架。由 LLM 智能体驱动的自主研究、分析、风险管理和交易角色。

description.en: On-device TTS model by Neuphonic. High-quality text-to-speech that runs entirely locally without cloud dependency.
description.zh: Neuphonic 推出的设备端 TTS 模型。无需依赖云端，完全本地运行的高质量文本转语音。

title.en: Musk vs Altman: 2017 Emails Reveal Altman Planned For-Profit Conversion Before 'Capped Profit' Announcement
title.zh: 马斯克 vs Altman：2017 年邮件显示 Altman 在宣布'封顶利润'前已计划营利转型

description.en: Day 3 of court proceedings revealed internal OpenAI emails from September 2017 showing Sam Altman and Greg Brockman discussing conversion to a for-profit entity months before the 'capped profit' structure was publicly announced in 2019.
description.zh: 法庭审理第3天披露的内部邮件显示，Sam Altman 和 Greg Brockman 在2017年9月就讨论过转为营利实体，比2019年公开宣布'封顶利润'结构早了好几个月。

insight.en: The 'smoking gun' isn't just that Altman planned profit early — it's that the entire 'capped profit' narrative may have been retrofitted to accommodate pre-existing commercial ambitions. If the court accepts this timeline, Microsoft's entire $10B+ investment structure and every enterprise contract OpenAI signed since 2019 rest on a potentially fraudulent foundation. The timing is brutal: OpenAI is racing toward an IPO while simultaneously defending its legal right to be a commercial entity at all. Goldman Sachs, reportedly already nervous after Q1 revenue misses, now faces the prospect of underwriting a company whose profit structure may be judicially invalidated. Meanwhile, Anthropic — structured as a public benefit corporation from day one — looks increasingly like the safer legal architecture.
insight.zh: '确凿证据'不仅仅是 Altman 很早就计划营利——而是整个'封顶利润'叙事可能是为了适应已有的商业野心而事后拼凑的。如果法院接受这一时间线，微软整个100亿+的投资结构以及 OpenAI 自2019年以来签署的每份企业合同都可能建立在潜在欺诈的基础上。时机非常残酷：OpenAI 一边冲刺 IPO，一边捍卫自己作为商业实体的合法权利。据报道，在 Q1 收入未达标后已经紧张的高盛，现在面临承销一家其利润结构可能被司法否定的公司的前景。与此同时，从第一天起就注册为公益公司的 Anthropic，在法律架构上看起来越来越安全。

title.en: OpenAI's IPO Advisors Threaten Withdrawal as Revenue Miss and Court Revelations Compound
title.zh: OpenAI 的 IPO 顾问威胁退出，收入缺口和法庭披露雪上加霜

description.en: Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley are reportedly reconsidering their roles as lead underwriters for OpenAI's planned IPO after the company missed Q1 revenue targets and the Musk trial revealed 2017 profit-planning emails.
description.zh: 据报道，在 OpenAI 未达成 Q1 收入目标且马斯克案披露了2017年营利计划邮件后，高盛和摩根士丹利正在重新考虑其作为 OpenAI 计划 IPO 主承销商的角色。

insight.en: The underwriting threat is the market's real-time risk assessment: if the banks pull out, OpenAI loses not just advisors but the credibility required for a public listing. This compounds the April 28 revenue miss (coding and enterprise markets lost to Anthropic) with a structural legal risk that could take quarters to resolve. The IPO window for 2026 was already narrowing — interest rate uncertainty and tech valuation compression haven't gone away. OpenAI now faces a three-front crisis: revenue underperformance vs Anthropic, legal existential risk from the Musk case, and a cooling IPO market. Compare this to Anthropic, which just secured up to $65B from Google and Amazon and is reportedly valued at ~$1 trillion on secondary markets. The funding narrative has fully inverted: OpenAI was the safe bet, Anthropic the challenger. That hierarchy is now in question.
insight.zh: 承销威胁是市场实时的风险评估：如果银行退出，OpenAI 失去的不仅仅是顾问，还有公开上市所需的信誉。这使4月28日的收入缺口（编码和企业市场输给 Anthropic）与可能需要数个季度才能解决的结构性法律风险叠加。2026年的 IPO 窗口已经在收窄——利率不确定性和科技估值压缩并未消失。OpenAI 现在面临三线危机：收入表现不及 Anthropic、马斯克案带来的法律存在性风险、以及冷却的 IPO 市场。相比之下，Anthropic 刚刚从 Google 和 Amazon 获得了最多650亿美元，据报道在二级市场的估值约为1万亿美元。融资叙事已经完全逆转：OpenAI 曾是被看好的押注，Anthropic 是挑战者。这种层级现在正受到质疑。

title.en: EU Issues First AI Act Fine — €800 Million on Meta for Unauthorized AI Training on User Data
title.zh: 欧盟开出 AI 法案首张罚单——Meta 因未经授权使用用户数据训练 AI 被罚8亿欧元

description.en: The European Commission fined Meta €800 million for using EU user data to train its AI models without explicit consent, marking the first major enforcement action under the EU AI Act. Meta's Model Capability Initiative (MCI) — which tracks employee keystrokes and mouse movements — was cited as evidence of systemic data overreach.
description.zh: 欧盟委员会因 Meta 未经明确同意使用欧盟用户数据训练其 AI 模型而对其处以8亿欧元罚款，标志着欧盟 AI 法案下的首次重大执法行动。Meta 的模型能力倡议（MCI）——追踪员工按键和鼠标移动——被引用为系统性数据越权的证据。

insight.en: The €800M figure is calibrated to hurt but not cripple — roughly 3% of Meta's quarterly revenue — and the Commission chose Meta deliberately as the highest-profile target for maximum deterrent effect. The MCI reference is significant because it connects the fine to Meta's dystopian employee surveillance revealed on April 23, painting a picture of a company that treats both employees and users as training data sources without consent. This fine lands three days after Geoffrey Hinton's UN 'brakeless car' warning and one day after the Musk trial exposed OpenAI's profit motives — creating a regulatory-reputational pincer on the entire US tech industry. The real signal: the EU AI Act is not theoretical anymore. Companies that assumed a 2-3 year grace period before enforcement are now recalculating compliance costs. For AI labs relying on broad data scraping for pre-training, this precedent is existential — especially if the Commission follows up with training-data transparency requirements.
insight.zh: 8亿欧元的金额经过精确校准——足以造成痛苦但不会瘫痪，约占 Meta 季度收入的3%——而且委员会故意选择 Meta 作为最高调的目标以实现最大的威慑效果。提及 MCI 很重要，因为它将罚款与4月23日曝光的 Meta 反乌托邦式员工监控联系起来，描绘了一幅公司将员工和用户都视为未经同意的训练数据来源的图景。这笔罚款在 Geoffrey Hinton 联合国'无刹车汽车'警告三天后、马斯克案曝光 OpenAI 营利动机一天后落地——对整个美国科技行业形成监管-声誉钳制。真正的信号是：欧盟 AI 法案不再是理论性的。那些假设在执法前有2-3年宽限期的公司现在正在重新计算合规成本。对于依赖广泛数据抓取进行预训练的 AI 实验室来说，这一先例是存在性的——尤其是如果委员会随后推出训练数据透明度要求。

title.en: GitHub Launches Copilot Agent Tier at $49 Per Agent/Month — First Major Platform to Monetize Agent-Scale Usage
title.zh: GitHub 推出 Copilot 智能体套餐，每位智能体每月49美元——首个对智能体规模使用收费的主要平台

description.en: GitHub announced a new Copilot Agent Tier pricing model at $49 per autonomous agent per month, capping free-tier agent usage at 500 commits/month. The move follows reports of 275 million commits per week and 17 million AI agent PRs in March 2026.
description.zh: GitHub 宣布新的 Copilot 智能体套餐定价模式，每位自主智能体每月49美元，将免费套餐的智能体使用量限制为每月500次提交。此举是在每周2.75亿次提交和2026年3月1700万次 AI 智能体 PR 的报道之后推出的。

insight.en: GitHub just solved its agent economics problem the way every platform eventually does: by charging for the resource that was previously free. The $49/agent/month pricing is deliberately calibrated against human developer costs ($10K+/month fully loaded) to make it look cheap while capturing the surplus value that agentic coding creates. The 500-commit free cap is the critical detail: it preserves the open-source illusion for hobbyists while forcing any serious agent deployment into paid tiers. This is a direct response to the April 27 revelation that GitHub Actions compute minutes hit 2.1 billion in a single week — the infrastructure economics were unsustainable. The secondary effect will be agent vendor consolidation: smaller agent frameworks that relied on GitHub's free tier will now face a $49/month per agent tax, favoring well-funded players like Cursor, Claude Code, and Codex. GitHub is essentially becoming a tollbooth on the agent economy it helped create.
insight.zh: GitHub 刚刚以每个平台最终都会采用的方式解决了其智能体经济问题：对以前免费的资源收费。每位智能体每月49美元的定价经过精心校准，相对于人类开发者成本（每月全负荷1万美元+）显得非常便宜，同时捕获了智能体编码创造的剩余价值。500次提交的免费上限是关键细节：它为爱好者保留了开源的假象，同时迫使任何严肃的智能体部署进入付费套餐。这是对4月27日披露的 GitHub Actions 单周计算分钟数达21亿的直接回应——基础设施经济是不可持续的。次要影响将是智能体供应商整合：依赖 GitHub 免费套餐的小型智能体框架现在将面临每位智能体每月49美元的税费，有利于资金雄厚的参与者如 Cursor、Claude Code 和 Codex。GitHub 实质上正在成为其帮助创造的智能体经济的高速公路收费站。

title.en: Mistral Ships Mistral Ultra 2 at 78.4% SWE-bench, €2/M Tokens — European Challenger Enters Enterprise Coding Race
title.zh: Mistral 发布 Mistral Ultra 2，SWE-bench 78.4%，每百万 token 2欧元——欧洲挑战者加入企业编码竞赛

description.en: Mistral AI released Mistral Ultra 2, scoring 78.4% on SWE-bench Verified at €2 per million tokens ($2.17/M). The model features 1M-token context, native MCP support, and EU data residency guarantees. Available on Mistral's own infrastructure and Azure AI Foundry.
description.zh: Mistral AI 发布 Mistral Ultra 2，在 SWE-bench Verified 上得分78.4%，每百万 token 2欧元（2.17美元）。该模型具备100万 token 上下文、原生 MCP 支持以及欧盟数据驻留保证。可在 Mistral 自有基础设施和 Azure AI Foundry 上使用。

insight.en: Mistral Ultra 2 is not trying to win benchmarks — it's trying to win procurement. At 78.4% SWE-bench, it sits comfortably above DeepSeek V4-Pro-Max (80.6%) and below Claude Opus 4.7 (87.6%) and GPT-5.5 (82.7%), but the €2/M pricing is the real weapon: 20% cheaper than DeepSeek's $1.74/M when adjusted for output quality, and an order of magnitude cheaper than Claude Opus 4.7's ~$10/M. The EU data residency guarantee is a feature no US lab can replicate easily — it directly addresses the compliance anxiety that the EU AI Act fine on Meta just amplified. The timing is opportunistic: European enterprises now face a US tech industry under regulatory-reputational siege (OpenAI's court case, Meta's fine, Google's $40B Anthropic bet raising EU antitrust eyebrows). Mistral is positioning itself as the geopolitically neutral, regulatorily compliant alternative. The question is whether 78.4% SWE-bench is good enough for enterprise workflows that have already tasted Claude Opus 4.7 and GPT-5.5 quality — procurement officers may find the price savings less compelling than the output quality gap.
insight.zh: Mistral Ultra 2 不是在试图赢得基准测试——而是在试图赢得采购。78.4% 的 SWE-bench 得分，它 comfortably 位于 DeepSeek V4-Pro-Max（80.6%）之下，低于 Claude Opus 4.7（87.6%）和 GPT-5.5（82.7%），但 €2/M 的定价才是真正的武器：按输出质量调整后比 DeepSeek 的 $1.74/M 便宜20%，比 Claude Opus 4.7 的约 $10/M 便宜一个数量级。欧盟数据驻留保证是美国实验室无法轻易复制的功能——它直接解决了欧盟 AI 法案对 Meta 的罚款刚刚加剧的合规焦虑。时机非常投机：欧洲企业现在面对的是一个处于监管-声誉围攻之下的美国科技行业（OpenAI 的法庭案件、Meta 的罚款、Google 对 Anthropic 的400亿美元投资引发欧盟反垄断关注）。Mistral 将自己定位为地缘政治中立、监管合规的替代方案。问题是78.4% 的 SWE-bench 对于已经体验过 Claude Opus 4.7 和 GPT-5.5 质量的企业工作流来说是否足够好——采购人员可能会发现价格节省不如输出质量差距有说服力。

title.en: TSMC Pauses New AI Chip Orders for Q3 After 3nm Yield Crisis Spreads to H200 Wafers
title.zh: 台积电在3nm良率危机蔓延至 H200 晶圆后暂停第三季度新 AI 芯片订单

description.en: TSMC has temporarily paused accepting new orders for AI accelerator chips scheduled for Q3 2026 delivery after a yield crisis in its 3nm process node spread from consumer chips to NVIDIA H200 and AMD MI350 wafers. Yield rates reportedly dropped from 75% to 52%.
description.zh: 台积电已暂时暂停接受预定2026年第三季度交付的 AI 加速器芯片新订单，此前其3nm工艺节点的良率危机从消费级芯片蔓延至 NVIDIA H200 和 AMD MI350 晶圆。据报道，良率从75%下降到52%。

insight.en: The 52% yield rate is catastrophic: it means nearly half of every H200 wafer is scrap, effectively doubling the cost per good die at a time when AI labs are already paying enormous premiums for compute. TSMC's pause on new orders creates a supply cliff for Q3 — NVIDIA, AMD, Google (TPU), and Amazon (Trainium) all have roadmap commitments that depend on TSMC's 3nm/4nm capacity. This is the hardware layer's revenge for the April 24 headline that Big Tech is spending $650B on AI infrastructure in 2026: you can't buy your way out of physics. The yield crisis also validates the neuromorphic chip research from Cambridge (April 24-28) that demonstrated 70% energy reduction — alternative silicon architectures look more compelling when conventional CMOS scaling hits yield walls. For OpenAI, already under pressure from revenue misses and IPO uncertainty, a Q3 compute shortage would mean higher inference costs or service degradation at exactly the wrong moment. Anthropic, with its $100B+ AWS commitment and Google's TPU pipeline, may be slightly more insulated — but only slightly.
insight.zh: 52% 的良率是灾难性的：这意味着几乎一半的 H200 晶圆都是废品，在 AI 实验室已经为算力支付巨额溢价的时刻，有效地将每颗合格芯片的成本翻倍。台积电暂停新订单造成了第三季度的供应悬崖——NVIDIA、AMD、Google（TPU）和 Amazon（Trainium）都有依赖于台积电 3nm/4nm 产能的路线承诺。这是硬件层对4月24日'科技巨头2026年在 AI 基础设施上支出6500亿美元'标题的反击：你无法用钱摆脱物理定律。良率危机也验证了剑桥大学4月24-28日的神经形态芯片研究，该研究证明了70%的能耗降低——当传统 CMOS 扩展遇到良率墙时，替代硅架构看起来更具吸引力。对于已经面临收入缺口和 IPO 不确定性的 OpenAI 来说，第三季度的算力短缺将意味着更高的推理成本或在完全错误的时刻出现服务降级。Anthropic 凭借其1000亿+美元的 AWS 承诺和 Google 的 TPU 管线，可能稍微更有缓冲——但也只是稍微。

title.en: Ineffable Intelligence Hires 50 Top Researchers from DeepMind, OpenAI, and Anthropic in 72 Hours
title.zh: Ineffable Intelligence 在72小时内从 DeepMind、OpenAI 和 Anthropic 挖走50名顶尖研究员

description.en: David Silver's Ineffable Intelligence has hired 50 senior researchers from DeepMind, OpenAI, and Anthropic within 72 hours of announcing its $1.1 billion seed round, including multiple authors of the AlphaGo, AlphaFold, and GPT-4 papers. The hires include former heads of alignment and safety teams.
description.zh: David Silver 的 Ineffable Intelligence 在宣布11亿美元种子轮融资后的72小时内，从 DeepMind、OpenAI 和 Anthropic 雇用了50名高级研究员，其中包括 AlphaGo、AlphaFold 和 GPT-4 论文的多位作者。被雇人员包括前对齐和安全团队负责人。

insight.en: A $1.1B seed round was already unprecedented; hiring 50 senior researchers in 72 hours is a declaration of intent that transcends normal startup behavior. The talent poaching is particularly damaging to Anthropic, which just lost its alignment team head at a moment when Claude Mythos 5's military-grade cybersecurity capabilities are drawing EU regulatory scrutiny and global safety concerns. OpenAI's losses are equally painful — the company is fighting a court case that questions its legal existence while trying to ship GPT-5.5 and prepare an IPO. DeepMind can absorb the hits better (it's Google-backed and massive), but the concentration of AlphaGo/AlphaFold/GPT-4 paper authors in one startup signals that Ineffable is assembling the most credentialed AI research team in history. The 'why now' question remains unanswered: Silver hasn't disclosed what they're building. But the caliber of hires — and their willingness to leave established labs for a pre-product startup — suggests the research direction is compelling enough to convince people who have seen everything. The concentration risk is real: if Ineffable fails, it could set back safety research across the industry. If it succeeds, it may redefine the competitive landscape faster than any incumbent can react.
insight.zh: 11亿美元的种子轮已经是史无前例的；在72小时内雇用50名高级研究员则是一种超越正常初创企业行为的意图宣言。人才挖角对 Anthropic 尤其有害，该公司刚刚失去了对齐团队负责人，而此时 Claude Mythos 5 的军事级网络安全能力正引起欧盟监管审查和全球安全担忧。OpenAI 的损失同样痛苦——该公司正在打一场质疑其法律存在性的官司，同时试图发布 GPT-5.5 并准备 IPO。DeepMind 可以更好地吸收打击（它有 Google 支持且规模庞大），但 AlphaGo/AlphaFold/GPT-4 论文作者集中在一家初创公司，表明 Ineffable 正在组建史上最有资历的 AI 研究团队。'为什么是现在'的问题仍未回答：Silver 尚未透露他们在做什么。但被聘用者的资历——以及他们愿意离开成熟实验室加入一家尚无产品的初创公司——表明研究方向足够吸引人，能够说服那些什么都见过的人。集中风险是真实的：如果 Ineffable 失败，可能会让整个行业的安全研究倒退。如果成功，它可能比任何现有企业都能更快地重新定义竞争格局。

insight_analysis.en:
April 29 marks the day when the AI industry's structural contradictions became impossible to ignore. The Musk vs Altman trial didn't just reveal 2017 profit-planning emails — it exposed the possibility that the entire 'capped profit' structure was a retrofit for pre-existing commercial ambition, threatening to invalidate Microsoft's $10B+ investment and every enterprise contract OpenAI has signed since 2019. Within hours, OpenAI's IPO advisors were reportedly threatening withdrawal — not because of a single bad headline, but because the legal foundation of the company itself is now in question, compounding the Q1 revenue miss that already showed Anthropic eating OpenAI's enterprise lunch.

Meanwhile, the EU chose this exact moment to issue its first AI Act fine — €800M on Meta for unauthorized training data use, citing the same Model Capability Initiative that tracks employee keystrokes and mouse movements. The regulatory pincer is real: US tech is simultaneously defending its profit structures in California courts and its data practices in Brussels, while Mistral positions itself as the geopolitically neutral European alternative.

At the infrastructure layer, TSMC's 3nm yield collapse to 52% is a physics-based reality check on the $650B Big Tech spending narrative — you cannot buy your way out of yield walls. This validates the neuromorphic research from Cambridge and raises the stakes for Q3 compute availability just as demand from agent-scale usage (17M AI PRs in March, 275M commits/week) hits exponential curves. GitHub's response — a $49/agent/month Copilot tier — is the first platform-level attempt to monetize agent economics, essentially becoming a tollbooth on the agent economy it helped create.

And then there's Ineffable: $1.1B seed, 50 senior researchers in 72 hours, multiple authors of AlphaGo, AlphaFold, and GPT-4 in one room. The concentration of talent is either the industry's greatest hope or its most dangerous concentration risk.

The pattern across all these events: the frameworks — legal, regulatory, physical, economic — are not keeping pace with capability deployment. OpenAI's legal structure may be invalidated by a court. Meta's data practices just cost it €800M. TSMC's physics limits just created a supply cliff. GitHub's free tier just hit a paywall. And a four-month-old startup just hired enough senior researchers to challenge any incumbent. The gap between what AI can do and what institutions can accommodate is widening faster than any previous technology transition. The companies betting on 'move fast and fix later' are now discovering that 'later' has arrived.
insight_analysis.zh:
4月29日标志着 AI 行业的结构性矛盾变得无法忽视的那一天。马斯克 vs Altman 的审判不仅仅揭示了2017年的营利计划邮件——还暴露了可能性：整个'封顶利润'结构是为已有商业野心而事后拼凑的，这威胁着要否定微软100亿+美元的投资以及 OpenAI 自2019年以来签署的每份企业合同。数小时内，据报道 OpenAI 的 IPO 顾问威胁退出——不是因为一个糟糕的标题，而是因为公司本身的法律基础现在受到质疑，这加剧了已经显示 Anthropic 正在蚕食 OpenAI 企业市场份额的 Q1 收入缺口。

与此同时，欧盟选择了这个精确时刻开出其首张 AI 法案罚单——因 Meta 未经授权使用训练数据而处以8亿欧元罚款，引用的正是追踪员工按键和鼠标移动的模型能力倡议（MCI）。监管钳制是真实的：美国科技行业同时在加利福尼亚法庭捍卫其利润结构，在布鲁塞尔捍卫其数据做法，而 Mistral 将自己定位为地缘政治中立的欧洲替代方案。

在基础设施层面，台积电3nm良率崩溃至52%是对6500亿美元科技巨头支出叙事的基于物理的现实检验——你无法用钱摆脱良率墙。这验证了剑桥大学的神经形态研究，并在智能体规模使用量（3月1700万次 AI PR，每周2.75亿次提交）呈指数曲线增长之际，提高了第三季度计算可用性的风险。GitHub 的回应——每位智能体每月49美元的 Copilot 套餐——是平台层面对智能体经济进行货币化的首次尝试，实质上正在成为其帮助创造的智能体经济的收费站。

然后是 Ineffable：11亿美元种子轮，72小时内50名高级研究员，AlphaGo、AlphaFold 和 GPT-4 的多位作者同处一室。人才的集中要么是行业最大的希望，要么是最危险的集中风险。

所有这些事件中的模式：框架——法律、监管、物理、经济——没有跟上能力部署的步伐。OpenAI 的法律结构可能被法院否定。Meta 的数据做法刚刚让它付出了8亿欧元。台积电的物理极限刚刚制造了供应悬崖。GitHub 的免费套餐刚刚撞上了付费墙。而一家成立四个月的初创公司刚刚雇用了足够多的高级研究员来挑战任何现有企业。AI 能做什么与机构能容纳什么之间的差距比以往任何技术转型都快地在扩大。那些押注'快速行动、稍后修复'的公司现在发现，'稍后'已经到来。
